prompt.original: Add a CSV export button to the orders table. It should respect the active filters and show a loading state while exporting.
prompt.understoodGoal: Add a filtered CSV export flow to the existing orders table with honest loading and failure states.

assumptions.assumption: The orders table already has a filter state object in the page component.
assumptions.reason: The existing table renders filtered rows and the export should reuse that source of truth.

assumptions.assumption: The export can be implemented as a frontend-triggered request to an existing API route.
assumptions.reason: No backend schema change was requested and the app already exposes order list data.

outOfScope: Changing the order table layout beyond adding the export action.

plan.title: Audit orders table and filter state
plan.description: Find the table component, filter state owner, and existing data loading pattern.

plan.title: Add export API call
plan.description: Create a small client helper that sends active filters to the export endpoint and downloads the returned CSV.

plan.title: Wire export button into toolbar
plan.description: Add an export button that uses the existing toolbar styling and shows loading and error states.

plan.title: Verify filtered export behavior
plan.description: Run tests if available and manually trace export with no filters, one filter, and empty results.

log.title: Audit orders table and filter state
log.what: Inspected the orders page, table toolbar, and data-fetching hook.

files.change: Read only; confirmed this component owns filter state.
Read only; confirmed toolbar already has secondary action styling.

log.result: Export belongs in the existing toolbar and should use the same filter state passed to the table query.
log.notes: No layout redesign needed. Keep the action visually secondary.

log.title: Add export API call
log.what: Added a client helper that posts current filters to the export endpoint and downloads the CSV blob.

files.change: Created helper for filtered CSV export and filename handling.

log.result: The export helper is ready to be called from the toolbar.
log.notes: The helper does not log filter payloads because filters can include private customer data.

log.title: Wire export button into toolbar
log.what: Started adding the export button to OrdersToolbar and passing active filters from OrdersPage.

files.change: In progress; export button shell added but error state is not wired yet.
In progress; active filters are being passed into the toolbar.

log.result: Toolbar integration is mid-flight. Do not run final verification until loading and error states are finished.
log.notes: Use the existing secondary button treatment. Do not introduce a new button variant.

lastPosition.lastAction: Added the export button shell and started passing active filters into OrdersToolbar.
lastPosition.nextStep: Finish OrdersToolbar loading and error handling, then verify the export helper receives the current filter state.
lastPosition.systemCondition: mid-flight - toolbar export button is partially wired and should not be considered complete.
criticalContext: Do not redesign the orders toolbar.
Do not log raw filter payloads because they may contain private customer data.
The export action should stay secondary, not become the primary page CTA.

decisions.decision: Reuse the existing orders filter state instead of creating separate export state.
decisions.reason: A separate state source could drift from the visible table results.

decisions.decision: Download the CSV blob on the client rather than navigating to the export URL.
decisions.reason: The UI needs an honest loading state and can show failure feedback without leaving the page.

completed: Audited the orders table and toolbar.
Added the filtered CSV export helper.
incomplete: Finish toolbar loading and error states.

filesChanged.change: Partially passed active filters to toolbar.

nextSessionMustKnow: Audit the current working tree before continuing because the interrupted file may have changed.
Do not commit runtime .runbook/sessions/SESSION-[timestamp].json files unless explicitly requested.
